questions.question: Derive the 2D rotation transformation matrix for rotating a point (x, y) by an angle θ about the origin.
Explain the three steps involved in rotating an object about a pivot point P(h, k) by angle θ.
keyPoints: Step 1: Translate object so pivot moves to origin using T(-h, -k)
Step 2: Apply rotation about origin using R(θ)
Step 3: Translate back using T(h, k)

questions.question: Prove that rotation is a rigid body transformation by showing it preserves distances.
keyPoints: Let points P1(x1,y1) and P2(x2,y2), distance d = √[(x2-x1)² + (y2-y1)²]
After rotation: x1' = x1cosθ - y1sinθ, y1' = x1sinθ + y1cosθ
Using trig identity cos²θ + sin²θ = 1, result equals original d²

questions.question: What is homogeneous coordinate representation? Write the 3x3 transformation matrix for 2D rotation.
keyPoints: Homogeneous coordinates add third dimension: (x, y, w) where w=1 for 2D

questions.question: Find the coordinates of point P(4, 3) after rotating by 90° counterclockwise about the origin.
keyPoints: Using formula: x' = x cos90° - y sin90°

questions.question: Explain why rotation matrix is orthogonal. Prove R(θ)^T = R(-θ).
keyPoints: Matrix R is orthogonal if R^T = R^-1

questions.question: What is the effect of rotating by 180° and 360°? Explain with examples.
keyPoints: 360°: (x, y) → (x, y). Returns to original position
180° is equivalent to point reflection through origin
360° is full rotation bringing object back to start
Note: 0° and 360° give same result (identity transformation)

questions.question: A triangle has vertices A(1,1), B(3,1), C(2,3). Find the coordinates of the triangle after rotation by 60° about the origin. Also find the matrix form of the transformation.
Explain rotation about an arbitrary point with a suitable example. Derive the combined transformation matrix and explain each component.
keyPoints: When rotating about pivot P(h,k), three steps: translate, rotate, translate back
R(θ) rotates about origin: [cosθ -sinθ 0; sinθ cosθ 0; 0 0 1]
Example: rotate point (5,5) about pivot (1,1) by 90°

questions.question: Discuss the properties of rotation transformation in computer graphics. Include in your answer: (i) Determinant value (ii) Inverse transformation (iii) Successive rotations (iv) Rigid body nature.
keyPoints: (ii) Inverse is R(-θ) = R(θ)^T, rotates in opposite direction
(iv) Rigid body: preserves distances, angles, parallelism, colinearity
Origin remains fixed when rotating about origin
CCW is positive direction by convention

questions.question: Write a program/pseudocode to rotate a point (x,y) by angle θ about the origin. Explain the algorithm and analyze its time complexity.
keyPoints: Algorithm: 1) Input x, y, θ 2) Convert θ to radians 3) Calculate x' = x*cos(θ) - y*sin(θ) 4) Calculate y' = x*sin(θ) + y*cos(θ) 5) Output (x', y')
Use trig functions from math library
For multiple points, iterate through each point
Time complexity: O(n) for n points, O(1) for single point
Space complexity: O(1) - only uses fixed variables
Can be optimized using rotation formulas for common angles (90°, 180°, 270°)
